planned_changes: Open ripple_root_cause_matrix.xlsx in a standard spreadsheet editor and re-save as a standard .xlsx with no macros or embedded objects to ensure openpyxl (or equivalent) can parse it in automated previews.

Add a new sheet named 'source_map' (or 'index') that lists every derived column, the exact cell ranges used, and a one-to-one mapping to input/task.json packet fields (e.g., Sheet1!G2:G5 -> input/task.json:/records:*.spool_error_pct).

Replace any hard-coded derived values with live formulas or add adjacent formula cells that explicitly show the formula (e.g., =A2 + 0.6*B2 - 0.25*C2) and ensure these cells are the ones referenced in the 'source_map'.

In corrective_action.md, add a dedicated 'Normalization and Units' subsection that documents any unit conversions or normalization factors, references the exact workbook cells (sheet and cell addresses), and justifies the chosen basis relative to the embedded packet.

Document threshold tie-break policy explicitly in the report (e.g., 'score > 70.0 => Fail; score == 70.0 => Warn') and include a short worked example showing classification at 70.0 and with +/-5% perturbations.

Document remediation-option interaction semantics (how overlapping reductions are combined), add per-record residual-risk formulas referencing remediation option cells, and show one recomputed end-to-end example for the recommended combo (O1+O2) applied to the release-controlling record.

Standardize numeric formatting/rounding in both worksheet display and the report (e.g., 3 decimal places) and ensure computed values in the workbook match those presented in corrective_action.md; correct any trivial presentation differences (e.g., 64.464 vs 64.4645).
Update final_message.txt to state that the workbook is now programmatically parseable, point to the 'source_map' sheet, and note that the requested examples and clarifications are included.
Re-run the deterministic precheck and verifier steps and confirm parse_status for the .xlsx is parseable and that the verifier's checklist items are satisfied.

raw_model_output.revision_reason: Address programmatic inspectability limitation and close minor documentation/discoverability gaps (normalization justification, score-matrix header mapping, tie-break policy, remediation interaction semantics, and rounding consistency) so verifiers can fully validate formulas and traceability.

metadata_json.revision_goal: Baseline delivered required artifacts and passed artifact-contract checks (final_score 0.845). The report and spreadsheet contain the scoring formula, per-record calculations, sensitivity analysis, remediation combos, and budget checks. Remaining issues are programmatic inspectability of the .xlsx (openpyxl_unavailable prevented formula-level verification) and a few discoverability/documentation gaps: explicit normalization/unit-conversion justification, a score-matrix header that maps spreadsheet columns to packet fields, tie-break policy at thresholds, and explicit remediation-interaction semantics for overlapping options. Minor numeric rounding presentation differences were observed but do not affect classifications. Manual opening of the workbook to confirm cell-level formulas and explicit cell-to-packet mappings is recommended for final verification.
